arts.ttl: Deal close for 125-career-goal star that Liverpool & Arsenal looked at
arts.sum: Leroy Sane is reportedly close to signing a new contract with Bayern Munich, despite Liverpool and Arsenal also looking at him, according to Christian Falk. The Germany international had been close to becoming a free agent this summer, but now looks set to extend his stay at the Allianz Arena. arts.ttl: Gabriel Jesus fires new Arsenal injury update as Mikel Arteta faces striker transfer dilemma
arts.sum: On Jesus, who sustained an similar injury to his opposite knee in 2022, and his transformation, Arteta added: "It's like going to the gym and looking in the mirror after one week or one month. Once the window shut, he said: "We had a clear intention which is always when a window opens for us to explore the opportunities to improve our squad with players that can make an impact.

arts.ttl: Man City handed new 115 charges timeline verdict as Arsenal, Chelsea and Tottenham wait goes on
arts.sum: It is now reported by the Telegraph that it is unlikely a final verdict will be given before the end of the current season or even before the summer. Manchester City and their Premier League rivals will have longer to wait for the verdict on the club’s 115 charges, according to latest reports.

arts.sum: Napoli are looking to strengthen in defence this summer and could turn to Arsenal’s Jakub Kiwior due to the injury status of Tottenham’s Radu Dragusin. Sources with a close understanding of the situation have informed CaughtOffside that Napoli are continuing to consider Dragusin as a target despite his complicated fitness situation. arts.sum: Tottenham Hotspur, Arsenal, Chelsea, Manchester City and Paris Saint-Gemain are all in the picture to sign West Ham United’s Mohammed Kudus. That’s according to Africa Foot, who state PSG face competition from Tottenham, Arsenal, Chelsea and Manchester City in the summer window. arts.ttl: Arsenal BOMBSHELL: Real Madrid use 'discreet' tactic to sign Saliba
arts.sum: Real Madrid have identified William Saliba as their priority centre-back target and are reportedly set to engage in “discreet” contact with the Arsenal star in a bid to sign him in 2026. Saliba, 24, has been heavily linked with a move to the Santiago Bernabeu over the past few months, but the France international is “highly unlikely” to join Los Blancos during the 2025 summer transfer window.

arts.ttl: Arsenal confirm two huge new pre-season friendlies as summer plans take shape
arts.sum: Gunners heading back to Singapore for pair of high-profile fixtures before facing Tottenham in Hong Kong
